sections.paragraph: m. pr. part, portion, number (see the root), specially mina, Gr. μνᾶ (Syr. ܡܰܢܝܐܳ, Arab. مَنُّ), the weight of a hundred shekels, as is gathered from 1Ki 10:17, compared with 2Ch 9:16.-The computation is obscure in the passage, Eze 45:12, “twenty shekels, twenty-five shekels, fifteen shekels shall be your maneh:” this must either be understood of a three-fold maneh, of twenty, twenty-five, and fifteen shekels, or else of one of sixty (15+20+25) shekels. But the former opinion is preferable.
